title: Parallel verify runner: scripts/run_verify.mjs (~90s -> ~30s)
action: Replace the sequential and-chain in .bee/config.json commands.verify with a new scripts/run_verify.mjs: a concurrency-capped pool (default 6) running all current verify suites, with the timing/lock-sensitive suites (test_store_lock, test_claim_race, test_reservation_race, test_state_write_concurrency, test_heartbeat_touch) grouped into ONE sequential unit so they never co-run with each other. Empirically -P16 flaked them on CPU contention; -P6 green 3/3, repo stays clean = contention not shared-state. Runner aggregates: any suite non-zero -> exit 1; prints per-suite PASS/FAIL + ms + total. Suite list derived to EXACTLY match current commands.verify. Update test_verify_manifest.mjs to read the mandatory-suite set from the runner instead of the and-string. Validate with at least 8 green runs.

truths: scripts/run_verify.mjs runs the EXACT same suite set as the previous commands.verify and-chain (identical coverage, checked against the old list).
Concurrency is capped (default 6, env-overridable); the 5 timing/lock suites run as one sequential unit so they never overlap each other.
Runner exits 0 if and only if EVERY suite passes; any single failure gives a non-zero exit naming the failing suite.
commands.verify becomes the single runner invocation; test_verify_manifest.mjs still guarantees every mandatory suite is present via the runner suite list.
Validated: at least 8 consecutive green runs, wall-time about 30s (down from about 90s).
prohibitions: Do NOT drop or rename any suite from the set.
Do NOT use unbounded/-P16 concurrency (proven flaky). Do NOT weaken any suite.

trace.verification_evidence: {"red_failure_evidence":"Before: .bee/config.json commands.verify was a 34-suite sequential and-chain taking ~90s (measured), dominated by test_onboard_bee 25s + test_bee_cli 16s (115 CLI spawns) + test_lib 15s, run one-at-a-time on 1 of 16 cores. Naive full -P16 parallelism = 29s but FLAKY (onboard_bee/claim_race intermittently trip on CPU-timing contention; working tree stays clean so it is contention not shared-state corruption).","green_evidence":"After: scripts/run_verify.mjs runs the identical 34-suite set (parity verified 34/34, MISSING/EXTRA both empty vs the old chain from git HEAD) in a concurrency-capped pool (default min(5,cpus), env BEE_VERIFY_CONCURRENCY), with the 5 timing/lock suites (store_lock, claim_race, reservation_race, state_write_concurrency, heartbeat_touch) run as one sequential unit. Exit 0 iff every suite passes (process.exit(anyFail?1:0), audited); entry-point guard so importing SUITES does not spawn. commands.verify set to node scripts/run_verify.mjs via bee config set; test_verify_manifest.mjs now reads mandatory suites from the runner SUITES export. Validated: worker 9/9 consecutive green + orchestrator 1 green = 10 green at concurrency=5, ~31-33s wall (down from ~90s, ~2.8x). release-manifest.json regenerated (test_verify_manifest.mjs is hash-pinned)."}
trace.verify_output: Orchestrator independent: run_verify exit=0 wall=32046ms; manifest PASS; suite parity old-vs-new 34/34 (MISSING=[], EXTRA=[]); exit-logic audited (process.exit(anyFail?1:0)). Worker 9/9 green at concurrency=5.
